name: RABBIT-FILE-BUG-28
title: list-items.py prints 'No items found' instead of 'no branch' guidance when filter passed and branch missing
description: list-items.py logic: when branch_ops.read_branch returns [], it prints either 'No items filed yet. Use /rabbit-file file' (when no filters) or 'No items found.' (when filters set). The discrimination is filter-based, not branch-existence-based. If origin/bug-backlog-files truly does not exist AND user passes --feature foo, output is 'No items found.' which misleadingly implies the branch exists. Spec says 'If branch missing, prints guidance to file first' - the script does not honor that invariant when filters are present. Fix: branch_ops.read_branch should distinguish 'branch missing' from 'no matches' (return None vs []), or list-items.py should call _branch_exists_on_remote directly.
